title: Common Misconceptions About the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)
summary: Explains why a WBS is not a schedule, cost estimate, or resource‑allocation tool and clarifies its true purpose as a deliverable‑oriented hierarchy.
content: Many stakeholders assume the Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) includes activities, timing, effort, or cost data. In fact, the WBS is strictly a hierarchical decomposition of project deliverables. It provides the foundation for detailed planning, but it does not contain schedules, budgets, or resource assignments. Treating the WBS as a schedule or cost estimate leads to incomplete planning and inaccurate forecasts.
can_answer: Is the Work Breakdown Structure a schedule?
Can I derive a cost estimate directly from a WBS?
Does the WBS assign resources to tasks?
